indicator_meta.Name: Gross national income (GNI) per capita
indicator_meta.Begruendung: Bruttonationaleinkommen pro Kopf: zentrales makroök. Einkommensmaß.

indicator_meta.Name: Labor force, total
indicator_meta.Beschreibung: Labor force comprises people ages 15 and older who supply labor for the production of goods and services during a specified period. It includes people who are currently employed and people who are unemployed but seeking work as well as first-time job-seekers. Not everyone who works is included, however. Unpaid workers, family workers, and students are often omitted, and some countries do not count members of the armed forces. Labor force size tends to vary during the year as seasonal workers enter and leave.
indicator_meta.Begruendung: Gesamtarbeitskräftepotenzial: misst das gesamtwirtschaftliche Arbeitsangebot.

indicator_meta.Name: Estimated gross national income per capita (Female)
indicator_meta.Begruendung: BNE pro Kopf (Frauen): geschlechtsspezifische Disaggregation des BNE.

indicator_meta.Name: Estimated gross national income per capita (Male)
indicator_meta.Begruendung: BNE pro Kopf (Männer): geschlechtsspezifische Disaggregation des BNE.

indicator_meta.Name: Labour force participation rate (Female)
indicator_meta.Begruendung: Erwerbsquote (Frauen): Anteil der Frauen, die dem Arbeitsmarkt zur Verfügung stehen.

indicator_meta.Name: Labour force participation rate (Male)
indicator_meta.Begruendung: Erwerbsquote (Männer): Anteil der Männer, die dem Arbeitsmarkt zur Verfügung stehen.

indicator_meta.Name: Net foreign assets
indicator_meta.Beschreibung: Net foreign assets are the sum of foreign assets held by monetary authorities and deposit money banks, less their foreign liabilities. Data are in current local currency.
indicator_meta.Begruendung: Netto-Auslandsvermögen monetärer Behörden: monetärer Bilanzindikator.

indicator_meta.Name: Energy intensity level of primary energy
indicator_meta.Beschreibung: Energy intensity level of primary energy is the ratio between energy supply and gross domestic product measured at purchasing power parity. Energy intensity is an indication of how much energy is used to produce one unit of economic output. Lower ratio indicates that less energy is used to produce one unit of output.
indicator_meta.Begruendung: Energieintensität (MJ/$BIP): misst Energieeinsatz pro Einheit Wirtschaftsleistung.

indicator_meta.Name: Unemployment, total (% of total labor force)
indicator_meta.Beschreibung: Unemployment refers to the share of the labor force that is without work but available for and seeking employment. Definitions of labor force and unemployment differ by country.
indicator_meta.Begruendung: Arbeitslosenquote: einer der drei Kernindikatoren der Makroökonomie (Mankiw, 2013, S. 5).

indicator_meta.Name: Employment to population ratio, 15+, total (%)
indicator_meta.Beschreibung: Employment to population ratio is the proportion of a country's population that is employed. Employment is defined as persons of working age who, during a short reference period, were engaged in any activity to produce goods or provide services for pay or profit, whether at work during the reference period (i.e. who worked in a job for at least one hour) or not at work due to temporary absence from a job, or to working-time arrangements. Ages 15 and older are generally considered the working-age population.
indicator_meta.Begruendung: Gesamtbeschäftigungsquote: misst den Anteil der erwerbstätigen Bevölkerung.

indicator_meta.Name: GDP per capita
indicator_meta.Beschreibung: GDP per capita is gross domestic product divided by midyear population. GDP is the sum of gross value added by all resident producers in the economy plus any product taxes and minus any subsidies not included in the value of the products. It is calculated without making deductions for depreciation of fabricated assets or for depletion and degradation of natural resources. Data are in constant 2015 U.S. dollars.
indicator_meta.Begruendung: BIP pro Kopf: meistgenutzter makroök. Wohlstandsindikator.

indicator_meta.Name: GDP
indicator_meta.Beschreibung: GDP at purchaser's prices is the sum of gross value added by all resident producers in the economy plus any product taxes and minus any subsidies not included in the value of the products. It is calculated without making deductions for depreciation of fabricated assets or for depletion and degradation of natural resources. Data are in constant 2015 prices, expressed in U.S. dollars. Dollar figures for GDP are converted from domestic currencies using 2015 official exchange rates. For a few countries where the official exchange rate does not reflect the rate effectively applied to actual foreign exchange transactions, an alternative conversion factor is used.
indicator_meta.Begruendung: Kernindikator der gesamtwirtschaftlichen Produktion (BIP).

indicator_meta.Name: Ores and metals imports
indicator_meta.Beschreibung: Ores and metals comprise commodities in SITC sections 27 (crude fertilizer, minerals nes); 28 (metalliferous ores, scrap); and 68 (non-ferrous metals).
indicator_meta.Begruendung: Rohstoffimporte Erze/Metalle (% Warenimporte): Außenhandelsstrukturindikator.

indicator_meta.Name: Agricultural raw materials imports
indicator_meta.Beschreibung: Agricultural raw materials comprise SITC section 2 (crude materials except fuels) excluding divisions 22, 27 (crude fertilizers and minerals excluding coal, petroleum, and precious stones), and 28 (metalliferous ores and scrap).
indicator_meta.Begruendung: Rohstoffimporte Agrar (% Warenimporte): Außenhandelsstrukturindikator.

indicator_meta.Name: Employment to population ratio, 15+, female (%)
indicator_meta.Beschreibung: Employment to population ratio is the proportion of a country's population that is employed. Employment is defined as persons of working age who, during a short reference period, were engaged in any activity to produce goods or provide services for pay or profit, whether at work during the reference period (i.e. who worked in a job for at least one hour) or not at work due to temporary absence from a job, or to working-time arrangements. Ages 15 and older are generally considered the working-age population.
indicator_meta.Begruendung: Beschäftigungsquote (Frauen): geschlechtsspezifischer Arbeitsmarktindikator.

indicator_meta.Name: GDP per person employed
indicator_meta.Beschreibung: GDP per person employed is gross domestic product (GDP) divided by total employment in the economy. Purchasing power parity (PPP) GDP is GDP converted to 2017 constant international dollars using PPP rates. An international dollar has the same purchasing power over GDP that a U.S. dollar has in the United States.
indicator_meta.Begruendung: BIP pro Beschäftigten: gesamtwirtschaftliche Arbeitsproduktivität.

indicator_meta.Name: General government final consumption expenditure
indicator_meta.Beschreibung: General government final consumption expenditure (formerly general government consumption) includes all government current expenditures for purchases of goods and services (including compensation of employees). It also includes most expenditures on national defense and security, but excludes government military expenditures that are part of government capital formation. Data are in current U.S. dollars.
indicator_meta.Begruendung: Staatlicher Endverbrauch: fiskalische Konsumkomponente des BIP.

indicator_meta.Name: Employment to population ratio, ages 15-24, total (%)
indicator_meta.Beschreibung: Employment to population ratio is the proportion of a country's population that is employed. Employment is defined as persons of working age who, during a short reference period, were engaged in any activity to produce goods or provide services for pay or profit, whether at work during the reference period (i.e. who worked in a job for at least one hour) or not at work due to temporary absence from a job, or to working-time arrangements. Ages 15-24 are generally considered the youth population.
indicator_meta.Begruendung: Beschäftigungsquote (15–24): Jugendarbeitsmarktindikator.

indicator_meta.Name: Adjusted savings: natural resources depletion
indicator_meta.Beschreibung: Natural resource depletion is the sum of net forest depletion, energy depletion, and mineral depletion. Net forest depletion is unit resource rents times the excess of roundwood harvest over natural growth. Energy depletion is the ratio of the value of the stock of energy resources to the remaining reserve lifetime (capped at 25 years). It covers coal, crude oil, and natural gas. Mineral depletion is the ratio of the value of the stock of mineral resources to the remaining reserve lifetime (capped at 25 years). It covers tin, gold, lead, zinc, iron, copper, nickel, silver, bauxite, and phosphate.
indicator_meta.Begruendung: Ressourcenraubbewertung (% BNE): Teil der volkswirtschaftlichen Nachhaltigkeitsbuchführung (Adjusted Net Savings).

indicator_meta.Name: High-technology exports
indicator_meta.Beschreibung: High-technology exports are products with high R&D intensity, such as aerospace, computers, pharmaceuticals, scientific Instruments, and electrical machinery. Data are in current U.S. dollars.
indicator_meta.Begruendung: Hochtechnologieexporte: misst Exportstruktur und Innovationsintensität.

indicator_meta.Name: Foreign direct investment, net outflows
indicator_meta.Beschreibung: Foreign direct investment refers to direct investment equity flows in an economy. It is the sum of equity capital, reinvestment of earnings, and other capital. Direct investment is a category of cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Ownership of 10 percent or more of the ordinary shares of voting stock is the criterion for determining the existence of a direct investment relationship. This series shows net outflows of investment from the reporting economy to the rest of the world, and is divided by GDP.
indicator_meta.Begruendung: Netto-Direktinvestitionen (% BIP): makroök. Kapitalflussindikator.

indicator_meta.Name: Exports of goods and services
indicator_meta.Beschreibung: Exports of goods and services represent the value of all goods and other market services provided to the rest of the world. They include the value of merchandise, freight, Insurance, transport, travel, royalties, license fees, and other services, such as communication, construction, financial, information, business, personal, and government services. They exclude compensation of employees and investment income (formerly called factor services) and transfer payments. Data are in constant 2015 prices, expressed in U.S. dollars.
indicator_meta.Begruendung: Exporte von Gütern und Dienstleistungen: Außenhandelskomponente der VGR.

indicator_meta.Name: Gross capital formation
indicator_meta.Beschreibung: Gross capital formation (formerly gross domestic investment) consists of outlays on additions to the fixed assets of the economy plus net changes in the level of inventories. Fixed assets include land improvements (fences, ditches, draIns, and so on); plant, machinery, and equipment purchases; and the construction of roads, railways, and the like, including schools, offices, hospitals, private residential dwellings, and commercial and industrial buildings. Inventories are stocks of goods held by firms to meet temporary or unexpected fluctuations in production or sales, and "work in progress." According to the 2008 SNA, net acquisitions of valuables are also considered capital formation. Data are in constant 2015 prices, expressed in U.S. dollars.
indicator_meta.Begruendung: Bruttoanlageinvestitionen: Investitionskomponente des BIP (VGR).

indicator_meta.Name: Government expenditure on education, total
indicator_meta.Beschreibung: General government expenditure on education (current, capital, and transfers) is expressed as a percentage of GDP. It includes expenditure funded by transfers from international sources to government. General government usually refers to local, regional and central governments.
indicator_meta.Begruendung: Staatliche Bildungsausgaben (% BIP): fiskalpolitischer Ausgabenindikator.

indicator_meta.Name: Net foreign assets per capita
indicator_meta.Beschreibung: Foreign assets minus foreign liabilities
indicator_meta.Begruendung: Netto-Auslandsvermögen pro Kopf: ökonomische Außenbilanzgröße.

indicator_meta.Name: Produced capital per capita
indicator_meta.Beschreibung: Value of machinery, buildings, equipment, and residential and nonresidential urban land
indicator_meta.Begruendung: Physischer Kapitalstock pro Kopf (Maschinen, Gebäude): ökonomische Vermögensgröße.
